blocks.content: The location was a swamp stretching to the horizon.
At first, everyone hovered in the air using Fly magic, but one by one, they inhaled the toxic gas rising from the swamp and fell into it.
The real challenge began then.
Students who fell into the swamp screamed in agony, their faces twisted beyond recognition.
It was undoubtedly excruciatingly painful.
The students still in the air gritted their teeth and endured.
But they all knew they would have to go in eventually.
The association conveyed through the survival program that the power of nature couldn't be overcome by magic. The strength to survive until the end lay in the magician's will, not their magic.
Students in the air began to vomit.
Those in the swamp were hit by it, but they were too overwhelmed to even realize something was falling on them.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Sigh.
Nade's sigh was heard. Slowly tilting, he looked back at Shirone and said, "See you later, Shirone."
As Nade fell, other students began to follow one by one.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yeah... Nade.
Students who failed the survival test crawled out of the Virtual Zone one by one.
With ice towels prepared in advance on their heads, they sat away from the device to rest.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Stage 3, Poisonous Swamp, has ended. Currently, there are 8 survivors on the red team and 7 on the blue team.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Hmm, more survived than expected? This year's senior class is certainly high-level.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Class Three has some strong students, and Fermi's group is still in. Of course, there are no weaklings in Class Two either.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Stage 4... 'Death Scythe,' right? This will tip the scales.
The difficulty level set by the Magic Association for certified magicians was Stage 4.
Here, they wouldn't just experience pain but actual death.
Whoooosh!
Hearing the wind roaring from the abyss, Shirone was endlessly falling.
If the previous stages were extreme natural environments, Stage 4 was a mechanical space.
It was the inside of a massive chimney with an immeasurable depth.
Hundreds of thousands of blades spun fiercely, leaving no gaps, slicing the students into pieces.
Death was something one couldn't adapt to.
Every time horrific and tragic emotions struck their minds, their Spirit Zones were destroyed, and dropouts increased.
Shirone gritted his teeth.
Had he been sliced and reconstituted about ten times? It was as if the program was asking, "Can you still endure this?"
A giant blade, like a tray, was waiting, leaving afterimages. There was probably no way to pass through it alive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Endure! Endure!'
Shirone instantly passed through the afterimage of the blade. However, his body was already divided into three pieces.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What words could describe the feeling of watching your legs fall before your face?
Even though people die, no one has experienced death, so human language will never find an accurate expression.
Other students were in the same situation.
Stabbed by blades protruding from the walls, they momentarily stopped falling, then continued their pitiful descent towards the next death as the blades retracted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haaah!
Reconstituted, Shirone gasped for air like a newborn taking its first breath.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Ugh! Just kill me, kill me!'
Seeing dozens of interlocking drills waiting like a mat, anyone would think that.
Thud!
Shirone fell there.
Whirr, whirr, whirr, thud!
And in an instant, his body was ground up and poured down like juice.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Damn it!
Nade woke up from the Virtual Zone, cursing.
Iruki, Amy, and even Fermi's group couldn't endure the Death Scythe stage and were thrown out, rolling on the ground.
Their heads felt like they were splitting, and nausea surged. Dry heaving and spitting out long strands of saliva were common.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This is crazy. Why do I always get stuck at Stage 4?
Amy's expression was grim as she wiped her lips.
Maintaining the Spirit Zone even after death was impossible without a powerful obsession that transcended life and death.
A seeker. A religious person. A murderer.
Whatever it was, from now on, it was a world of mental realms beyond ordinary people.
So it wasn't strange that Iruki had dropped out.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Dead is dead. What's next?
From the start, one shouldn't create situations where they would die.
His mind, which hated uncertainty, couldn't accept this stage.
The Death Scythe stage met the association's expectations perfectly.
Many dropouts occurred at Stage 4, and most crawled out of the Virtual Zone looking like death warmed over.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ugh, I'm dying. Hey, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Nade, massaging his neck, approached Iruki. Iruki's eyes changed to a puzzled look as he turned to him.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Don't tell me, you dropped out too?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yeah. My head's killing me.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Why?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Why? What do you mean?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Maybe it's a natural result?
Nade didn't think about what came after death. It was probably a deliberate suppression.
If his mind transcended death, it would be impossible for him to remain a normal human being.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Never mind. Let's get out of here. Dying too much is killing me.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Entering Stage 5, Insect King, in 30 seconds. Shall we open the vision?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hmm, Insect King...
Having passed the 'Death Scythe,' the standard for professional magicians, they were now entering a world that couldn't be defined by theory.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Open the vision.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Yes. Activating the master device.
As Collie operated the device, a large hologram curved concavely appeared in the air of the Virtual Zone.
In the graduation exam, a giant hologram appears on the ceiling from the start, but the current vision also allowed a clear view of the program's interior.
-Entering Stage 5, Insect King, in the extreme cold program.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Ugh! What's that!
When the Insect King scene appeared on the vision, the dropouts gathered in front of the Virtual Zone recoiled in horror.
Countless disgusting insects were writhing endlessly. The sight of billions of insects wriggling made it look like the ground was moving.
The survivors were probably somewhere in there, buried in insects and suffering.
Stage 5, Insect King.
It was a place filled with fear, disgust, and pain so intense that death seemed preferable.
The sight of insects writhing together provoked primal human fear, but this time, it wasn't just watching.
They penetrated the skin, nerves, and organs, transmitting real sensations.
The pain of insects burrowing into every hole in the body and eating the flesh didn't end with death.
As those who couldn't endure it increased, the insect-covered ground began to stir.
Someone, covered in insects like a blackened corpse, suddenly stood up and started thrashing and screaming.
Ahhh!
Aider, lying in the Virtual Zone, echoed the scream from the simulation.
Ahhh! Ahhh! Ahhh!
Thrashing and rolling on the ground, he flailed his limbs, trying to crawl somewhere.
No one helped him. Approaching someone in severe shock could cause even greater trauma.
Still, perhaps due to the talent that got him through the Death Scythe stage, Aider quickly regained his composure.
Huff! Huff!
On the boundary between virtual and reality, he looked around in terror, tears welling up.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Ah, this is really scary.
How could it not be?
Those who dropped out at the Death Scythe stage felt relieved that it ended there after seeing Aider's state.
On the other hand, Screamer's expression was grim.
He had brought Aider to the blue team because he had scored well in the survival evaluation since last year.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Aider couldn't endure it, but Shirone can?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: That kid Aider, he made it to Stage 5? Not bad for someone so young.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's the youngest in the senior class. Maybe not in skill, but definitely in talent.
The difficulty of the Insect King stage was incomparable to the Death Scythe.
Starting with Aider, students began to drop out one by one.
Canis grimaced as he sat up, and shortly after, Arin slowly opened her eyes.
Unlike Aider, they calmly collected themselves.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: More or less. It's much harder than expected. The Spirit Zone becomes too sensitive after Stage 4.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: There are too many insects secreting toxins. It's hard to maintain equanimity when they're stinging your organs.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Talking about equanimity... these guys are nuts.
Arin's assessment was fitting for a mental specialist, but those who dropped out before the Death Scythe stage were bewildered.
It was only natural that talking about toxins and equanimity while insects were tearing through their intestines made them seem insane.
But that's why it's called a state of mastery.
The depth of this realm, which cannot be measured by numbers, is a sensation that cannot be understood without reaching it.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Everyone is amazing.
While the dropouts gathered in the Virtual Zone, Maya remained in the resting area.
She was the first to drop out, but her recovery was the slowest, and no one paid attention to her.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Amy, are you okay? How's your mind?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah. I think we're okay. How about you?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm feeling better now. I'm sorry. I dropped out at Stage 2.
Maya lowered her head, feeling ashamed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's okay. Honestly, I was really shocked too. Don't worry too much.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But... is Shirone okay? He hasn't come out yet.
Amy looked at the Virtual Zone. Everyone had dropped out at Stage 5, and only two people were left lying there.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: At least the balance is maintained until the end. Whoever wins there will win overall.
Maya looked at the vision floating in the air.
It was disgusting even from a distance, but seeing the screen up close made her feel nauseous again.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: How can they endure that?
Stage 2, Ice Prison, was terrifying enough for her.
Yet someone was fighting in such a horrific place.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: If it were me, I would have just died.
Maya couldn't bear to watch the vision.
But she didn't look away because Shirone, who believed in her, was fighting.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Th-there...!
Boyle pointed at the vision with a horrified look.
The insects wriggled, and Shirone slowly stood up.
Oh my god...
Maya's face turned pale.
Other students were also shocked, staring at the vision with their mouths open.
Shirone's appearance was unbearable to look at. Some parts of his body were stripped to the bone, and other parts were swollen and rotting.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: With his strength completely drained, Shirone hung his head with his arms limp.
Even as insects tore through his skin, he didn't move, and Alpheus's lips curled into a smile.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Impressive. Perfect equanimity.
It wasn't that he didn't feel pain or disgust. It was a superhuman state of maintaining focus in any situation.
